title: Flagship event to celebrate best of county
content:
A new flagship event to celebrate Northamptonshire's talent, history, countryside,  business and culture is to take place this autumn.

West and Northamptonshire Councils said they were working on the "first county-wide cultural event" to mark Northamptonshire Day on 25 October.

They said, along with Discover Northamptonshire, they would present the "bold" event at Northampton's Market Square, where a £12m renovation was completed last year.

The day would showcase "everything that makes the county special - from local talent and rich heritage to delicious food and family-friendly fun", they added.

Mark Arnull, Reform UK leader of West Northamptonshire Council, said it was "about celebrating the civic pride we all feel for where we live and work, from our historic towns and stunning countryside to the people, businesses and culture that make our communities thrive".

He added: "This is more than just a day of events; it's the start of a shared tradition, rooted in civic pride and future ambition."

Martin Griffiths, Reform UK leader of North Northamptonshire Council, said: "I was born and raised in the county and am extremely proud to call Northamptonshire my home - it is an exceptional area of the country.

"We are committed to nurturing community spirit and spotlighting local strengths, and this event is a fantastic step forward in that journey."

Richard Clinton, chairman of Discover Northamptonshire, said: "It's a moment to shine a light on our heritage, creativity, and visitor offer while giving residents and guests the chance to explore hidden gems and shared stories."

It would also show the county as a "must-visit destination to all", he added.

The event will take place from 13:00 BST to 19:30 and will help mark a year since the regeneration of the Market Square was completed.
